sections.heading: The objects of this Act are—
sections.title: Objects of the Act
sections.summary: This section outlines the purposes of the Act, including promoting constitutional rights to access information, balancing privacy with transparency, and ensuring public and private bodies operate with accountability and transparency.

requirements.text: To give effect to the constitutional right of access to information held by the State
To give effect to the constitutional right of access to information held by another person required for rights exercise or protection
To balance the right with other rights, including constitutional rights
To promote human rights culture and social justice through public bodies
To establish mechanisms for swift, inexpensive, and effortless access to records
To promote transparency, accountability, and effective governance

sections.source_quote: 9. The objects of this Act are— (a) to give effect to the constitutional right of access to— (i) any information held by the State; and (ii) any information that is held by another person and that is required for the exercise or protection of any rights; (b) to give effect to that right— (i) subject to justifiable limitations, including, but not limited to, limitations aimed at the reasonable protection of privacy, commercial confidentiality and effective, efficient and good governance; and (ii) in a manner which balances that right with any other rights, including the rights in the Bill of Rights in Chapter 2 of the Constitution; (c) to give effect to the constitutional obligations of the State of promoting a human rights culture and social justice, by including public bodies in the definition of "requester", allowing them, amongst others, to access information from private bodies upon compliance with the four requirements in this Act, including an additional obligation for certain public bodies in certain instances to act in the public interest; (d) to establish voluntary and mandatory mechanisms or procedures to give effect to that right in a manner which enables persons to obtain access to records of public and private bodies as swiftly, inexpensively and effortlessly as reasonably possible; and (e) generally, to promote transparency, accountability and effective governance of all public and private bodies by, including, but not limited to, empowering and educating everyone— (i) to understand their rights in terms of this Act in order to exercise their rights in relation to public and private bodies; (ii) to understand the functions and operation of public bodies; and (iii) to effectively scrutinise, and participate in, decision-making by public bodies that affects their rights.

sections.title: Fee payment requirements for non-personal record access requests
sections.summary: The information officer must require non-personal requesters to pay a prescribed request fee before processing their access requests. If the search or preparation of the record would take more time than prescribed, the officer must require a deposit of up to one-third of the potential access fee. The notice must specify the deposit amount, appeal options, and procedures for lodging appeals. If a deposit is paid for a refused request, it must be repaid. The officer must withhold records until fees are paid. Requesters granted access must pay fees for reproduction and search time beyond prescribed hours.

sections.heading: If no provision for the correction of personal information in a record of a public
sections.title: Correction of personal information in records
sections.summary: If no provision for correcting personal information in a public or private body's record exists, that body must take reasonable steps to establish internal measures for correction until legislation providing for such correction takes effect.
sections.dtia_summary: This section requires public and private bodies to implement correction mechanisms for personal information, which could impact DTIAs when data is transferred or stored across borders.

requirements.text: Public or private bodies must take reasonable steps to establish adequate and appropriate internal measures for correcting personal information

sections.source_quote: 88. If no provision for the correction of personal information in a record of a public or private body exists, that public or private body must take reasonable steps to establish adequate and appropriate internal measures providing for such correction until legislation providing for such correction takes effect.

sections.heading: No person is criminally or civilly liable for anything done in good faith in the
sections.title: Good faith immunity for PAIA actions
sections.summary: This section provides immunity from criminal or civil liability for individuals acting in good faith when exercising or performing powers or duties under the Act. It explicitly covers actions taken in good faith during the exercise of any power or duty defined by the Act. The immunity applies to all persons who comply with the Act's requirements without intent to violate it.

requirements.text: No person is criminally or civilly liable for anything done in good faith in the exercise or performance or purported exercise or performance of any power or duty in terms of this Act

sections.source_quote: 89. No person is criminally or civilly liable for anything done in good faith in the exercise or performance or purported exercise or performance of any power or duty in terms of this Act.

sections.heading: A person who with intent to deny a right of access in terms of this Act—
sections.title: Penalties for obstructing PAIA access rights
sections.summary: This section defines offences for intentionally denying access rights under the Act. It prohibits destroying, concealing, or falsifying records to obstruct access. Offenders face fines or imprisonment up to two years upon conviction. The offences apply to any person who intentionally interferes with records required for PAIA compliance.

requirements.text: A person who with intent to deny a right of access in terms of this Act destroys, damages or alters a record
A person who with intent to deny a right of access in terms of this Act conceals a record
A person who with intent to deny a right of access in terms of this Act falsifies a record or makes a false record

sections.source_quote: 90. A person who with intent to deny a right of access in terms of this Act— (a) destroys, damages or alters a record; (b) conceals a record; or (c) falsifies a record or makes a false record, commits an offence and is liable on conviction to a fine or to imprisonment for a period not exceeding two years.
sections.relevance_reason: Penalties for obstructing PAIA access rights

sections.heading: Section 6 of the Public Protector Act, 1994 (), is hereby
sections.title: Amendment to Public Protector Act for PAIA dispute resolution
sections.summary: This section amends Section 6 of the Public Protector Act to add a new duty for the Public Protector to resolve disputes by mediation, conciliation, negotiation, or other means when a complaint arises under the Act. The amendment requires the Public Protector to act on their own initiative to resolve disputes related to the Promotion of Access to Information Act, 2000. The amendment does not create new obligations but modifies existing procedures.

requirements.text: The Public Protector must, on his or her own initiative, on receipt of a complaint or on request relating to the operation or administration of the Promotion of Access to Information Act, 2000, endeavour in his or her sole discretion to resolve any dispute by mediation, conciliation or negotiation

The Public Protector must, on his or her own initiative, on receipt of a complaint or on request relating to the operation or administration of the Promotion of Access to Information Act, 2000, advise where necessary any complainant regarding appropriate remedies

The Public Protector must, on his or her own initiative, on receipt of a complaint or on request relating to the operation or administration of the Promotion of Access to Information Act, 2000, use any other means that may be expedient in the circumstances

sections.source_quote: 91. Section 6 of the Public Protector Act, 1994 (), is hereby amended— (a) by the substitution in paragraph (c) of subsection (4) for the expression "authority." of the expression "authority; and"; and (b) by the addition to subsection (4) of the following paragraph: (d) on his or her own initiative, on receipt of a complaint or on request relating to the operation or administration of the Promotion of Access to Information Act, 2000, endeavour, in his or her sole discretion. to resolve any dispute by— (i) mediation, conciliation or negotiation; (ii) advising, where necessary, any complainant regarding appropriate remedies; or (iii) any other means that may be expedient in the circumstances.

sections.heading: (1) The Minister may, by notice in the Gazette, m~e regulations regarding—
sections.title: Regulation-making authority for PAIA
sections.summary: This section grants the Minister the authority to make regulations for matters required or permitted by the Act, including fees, notices, uniform criteria for record availability, and administrative matters. Regulations must be submitted to Parliament before publication. Regulations affecting state finances must involve consultation with the Minister of Finance. The section does not create direct obligations but outlines the regulatory framework for implementation.

requirements.text: The Minister may make regulations regarding any matter required or permitted by this Act to be prescribed

The Minister may make regulations regarding any notice required by this Act
The Minister may make regulations regarding uniform criteria for information officers to decide which categories of records are made available under section 15
The Minister may make regulations regarding administrative or procedural matters necessary to give effect to the provisions of this Act
Any regulation must be submitted to Parliament before publication in the Gazette
Regulations affecting state finances must be made with consultation from the Minister of Finance

sections.source_quote: 92. (1) The Minister may, by notice in the Gazette, make regulations regarding— (a) any matter which is required or permitted by this Act to be prescribed; (b) any matter relating to the fees contemplated in sections 22 and 54; (c) any notice required by this Act; (d) uniform criteria to be applied by the information officer of a public body when deciding which categories of records are to be made available in terms of section 15; and (e) any administrative or procedural matter necessary to give effect to the provisions of this Act. (2) Any regulation in terms of subsection (1) must, before publication in the Gazette, be submitted to Parliament. (3) Any regulation in terms of subsection (1) which— (a) relates to fees; or (b) may result in financial expenditure for the State, must be made by the Minister acting in consultation with the Minister of Finance,
